title: Classic Comfort Algerian Sirloin Steak Tagine
description: A pungent and tender Algerian dish featuring sirloin steak, render fat, and a blend of spices, perfect for a midnight craving. This buddhist-temple-friendly recipe is free from animal by-products and harsh ingredients.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the cubed sirloin steak and cook until it is browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the chopped dried apricots and chicken broth, and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the Dutch oven, and simmer the tagine for 1.5 hours, or until the steak is tender.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
